entry.title: Re: Welcome all SAP Community members, start here!
entry.content:
A very warm welcome @Arun2024 - great to have you in SAP Community! You won't get tricked if you regularly visit the Products and Technology boards and the Topic pages (get to know about SAP products)!!

I'd like to share some other interesting links with you:

  • Blog posts (read what other community members have to say)
  • Discussion groups (discuss, connect, share)
  • What's New (stay up-to-date with what's new in SAP Community)

entry.title: Re: I created an advanced excel file with abap ole object...
entry.content:
You can upload the file and attach it to the mail, or use OLE2 to send the mail (with a local mail client such as Outlook).

What did you already try?

entry.title: Re: CAP subscribe to multiple channel with SAP Event Mesh client
entry.content: This is not possible. You need to create multiple CAP services instead and use composite messaging.

entry.title: Re: Latest Goal Management - Character Limitation on Name and Metric
entry.content:
We have just turned on the new goal management in our test system and we incredibly disappointed that this new "feature" has not been communicated by SAP properly. There's no good one source of information from SAP. Instead you have to find random influence tickets, blog posts, help documentation which we did. Even then we didn't come across anything about the reduced character limit.

Does anyone know if you leave old goal plans not "refreshed" in November will they automatically upgrade and everyone will lose half their historical objectives?! Or could we live everything historical not upgraded and lose nothing?

entry.title: sap hana certification
entry.content:
Hi all,
 
I want to appear for the C_HANAIMP_18 exam. However, I have noticed that it will expire by 28th March 2024. Since there is not enough time to prepare for the exam, I don't want to register before 28th March. I am unable to find any other certifications suitable for SAP Hana after that. Does anyone know if any other certifications related to SAP Hana will be available from April. Thanks in advance.
 
Regards
Priyanka

entry.title: Re: How to restrict MMBE and MB52 transactions with SLOC...
entry.content: @Dinesh_kaja Please coordinate with your basis consultant for role and authorization object M_MSEG_LGO

entry.title: Re: create a new product catalog sync that perform only s...
entry.content:
If your prices are coming from an external source then do you really need them to be catalog version aware? In more recent versions of SAP Commerce prices are decoupled from products and linked by a 'soft' relationship on product code.

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_COMMERCE_CLOUD_PUBLIC_CLOUD/aa417173fe4a4ba5a473c93eb730a417/8ba99c3b86691014913da449dde2783a.html?locale=en-US

You can have you prices imported from an external source and they immediately become the current price for products in both the staged and online catalogs.

To answer your actual question, I'm not really sure what you are trying to achieve with your SyncAttributeDescriptor config but the attributeDescriptor needs to define a valid attribute. There is no 'products' attribute on the 'PriceRow' type. If I understand your question correctly, I think you'd be better using the 'rootTypes' attribute on the CatalogVersionSyncJob to just sync PriceRow types and potentially mark the 'product' (note no 's') attribute as includedInSync=false so that it doesn't try and sync the linked product.

entry.title: Re: Attachment in packspec in EWM
entry.content:
Hi Prabakaran Gurusamy, 

were you able to find a possible solution, because I have the same case. 

Thank you in advance!

Dilan

entry.title: Artificial Intelligence and the Energy Transition
entry.content:
AI has long been used in the industry to analyze vast amounts of subsurface data, enabling companies to increase the amount of oil or gas that can be recovered. However, its impact extends far beyond that. AI is also being utilized to reduce the demand for fossil fuels by transforming the transportation industry. By optimizing routes, predicting maintenance needs, and improving fuel efficiency, AI is helping to make transportation more sustainable and environmentally friendly.

Furthermore, AI is being employed throughout the energy value chain to reduce costs and improve productivity. By automating complex tasks, AI systems can enhance operational efficiency and reduce the risk of accidents. For example, AI-powered predictive maintenance systems can identify potential equipment failures before they occur, allowing for timely repairs and minimizing downtime.

One notable development in the AI space is SAP's cloud enterprise portfolio, which includes a natural-language, generative AI copilot called "Joule." While not specifically designed for the oil, gas, and energy industry, Joule can analyze data from multiple systems and provide proactive and contextualized insights. This technology has the potential to benefit the industry by improving decision-making and driving operational excellence.

I recently had the pleasure of attending an Americas SAP Users Group (ASUG) event in Dallas, Texas, and while there, met with three AI startups that all are SAP partners and have a promising future in driving the energy transition in the oil, gas, and energy sector

Evolution Energie, a promising startup in the energy sector, is making waves with its innovative solutions. With a focus on sustainable energy production and consumption, the company is revolutionizing the way we power our lives. By harnessing the potential of renewable sources, Evolution Energie is paving the way for a greener and more sustainable future. Their cutting-edge technologies and forward-thinking approach have garnered attention from investors and industry experts alike. As the demand for clean energy continues to rise, Evolution Energie is well-positioned to lead the charge in transforming the energy landscape. With their commitment to innovation and sustainability, this startup is poised to make a significant impact in the years to come.  Their lighthouse solution is “Flexinergy”, which is an energy profitability and sustainability software solution used by some impressive major energy players, including TotalEnergies.

Shyftplan is changing the way businesses manage their workforce. This innovative startup offers a comprehensive solution for shift planning, time tracking, and employee communication. With its user-friendly interface and powerful features, Shyftplan streamlines the scheduling process, allowing businesses to save time and reduce administrative overhead. The platform also enables employees to easily view their schedules, request time off, and communicate with their team, fostering a more collaborative and efficient work environment. Whether a small business or a large enterprise, Shyftplan provides the tools needed to optimize workforce management.  Shyftplan has one major energy company who reduced managers’ efforts for shift scheduling by 60-80%.  Shyftplan is available on the SAP Store.

Everstream Analytics is an exciting new startup that is revolutionizing the way businesses analyze and understand their data. With their advanced artificial intelligence technology, Everstream Analytics enables companies to gain valuable insights and make data-driven decisions with ease. Their platform offers powerful data visualization tools, predictive analytics, and machine learning capabilities, all designed to help businesses unlock the full potential of their data. Whether it's optimizing operations, identifying customer trends, or predicting market behavior, Everstream Analytics empowers organizations to stay ahead of the competition by providing risk intelligence from a data layer that includes weather, pending strikes, etc., not typically considered when making supply chain decisions.

Sustainable energy transition is transforming the oil, gas, and energy industry, and artificial intelligence will be a key driver in areas such as improving energy efficiency, generating perfect work schedules with ease, and providing risk intelligence to traditional supply chain decision making. The adoption of AI technologies holds immense potential to increase efficiency, reduce costs, and pave the way towards a sustainable and greener future.

entry.title: Re: KMAT with KMAT in Bill of Material
entry.content:
Hi,

Kindly refer to these blogs,

From a business perspective variant configuration in simple steps 

End to End Process Document for Variant Configuration 

Regards

entry.title: Workflow for Purchase requisition through MRP
entry.content:
Hi,

I have already created and configured workflow for purchase requisition and activated, When I create PR manually, workflow is triggering. But when i create PR through MRP, workflow is not getting triggered. Kindly provide a solution regarding this issue.


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: Workflow for Purchase requisition through MRP
entry.content:
Please check the attached KBA which esplains the details :3422280

  • Due to Performance reasons the Purchase Requisitions created by MRP are not relevant for the Approval Process.
  • A later change on those PR will move them to the approval process in case  the start conditions are met.
  • The change on the PR can be done manually, assigning a Source of Supply or through the Hand Over Process in the MRP side. 
  • The Hand Over Process in the MRP area will move the Purchase requisitions to the Approval Process if the Start conditions are met. It can be executed individually or in Mass.

Thanks 

SAP Support

entry.title: Re: MF60
entry.content: you want default issue storage location?

entry.title: Re: Fiori Elements: OVP Donut's card
entry.content: In the code the propertypath values are missing. I am currently working with https://sapes5.sapdevcenter.com/sap/opu/odata/sap/SEPMRA_PROD_MAN . I find it difficult to create a donut chart with the given dataset. It is requested to help me out in choosing the entityset with appropriate property paths.

entry.title: Partner Innovation - eMudhra's Co-Innovation Journey with SAP
entry.content:
eMudhra is a leading digital identity and transaction management company specializing in secure and efficient digital transformation solutions. At its core, eMudhra provides a wide array of solutions including digital signatures, eSignature and document workflow platform, encryption certificates, and authentication services. Among its flagship offerings, emSigner stands out as a revolutionary digital signing and workflow management tool that seamlessly integrates with enterprise platforms like SAP S/4 HANA and SuccessFactors. This integration enables businesses to digitize and automate their document-centric processes, ensuring compliance, enhancing security, and improving operational efficiency.

"We embarked on our journey with SAP's Partner Innovation Lifecycle Services (PILS) with a vision to leverage SAP's vast technological ecosystem to accelerate our innovation journey. The Co-Innovated with SAP service has been instrumental in fine-tuning emSigner to seamlessly integrate with SAP technologies, enhancing our product's capabilities and reach. This collaboration has not only expedited our development cycle but has also enriched our offerings, allowing us to deliver more value to our customers. We're grateful for this partnership, which has been a catalyst for our growth and innovation." – Kaushik Srinivasan, Co-Founder, eMudhra.

“PILS has been working with eMudhra for the past 4 years in helping them bring innovations around digital signature solutions, in compliance with local regulations.  eMudhra being a Certified Authority in India and other geographies bring a unique value with their paperless platform – “emSigner, powered by AI”, to our customers both in SAP S/4HANA Cloud and SAP SuccessFactors.  eMudhra will continue to innovate and bring more solutions to SAP customers to embark on a paperless digital transformation.  We value our relationship with eMudhra and wish them a very successful journey with SAP and its customers” – Nandagopal B Prasad, SVP and Head, Partner Innovation Lifecycle Services, SAP.

emSigner, designed for both SAP S/4 HANA and SuccessFactors, addresses several critical business challenges:

  • Compliance and Security: Ensures that digital transactions meet global, legal and regulatory requirements, providing a secure and compliant environment for digital signatures and document management.
  • Operational Efficiency: Automates and streamlines document-centric processes, reducing manual tasks, and improving turnaround times.
  • Cost Reduction: Minimizes expenses related to paper-based processes, including printing, shipping, and storage costs.
  • Enhanced User Experience: Provides an intuitive interface for digital document management, simplifying workflows for users and improving overall satisfaction.
  • Remote Work Enablement: Facilitates secure and efficient remote document processing, critical in today’s increasingly digital and mobile workforce.

The solutions “emSigner for SAP S/4HANA Cloud” and “emSigner for SAP SuccessFactors” are available in SAP Store as well.

 

eMudhra began its journey with SAP IN 2019, focusing on integrating its digital signature and transaction management solutions with SAP’s enterprise software ecosystem. This collaboration aimed to leverage SAP's powerful platforms to enhance document security and workflow automation for SAP users globally. The partnership has been built on a shared vision of digital transformation and operational excellence.

Our experience working with SAP's Partner Innovation Lifecycle Services (PILS) on our Co-Innovation journey spans over 4 years. This engagement has been immensely valuable, providing us with insights, resources, and support needed to tailor our solutions for SAP environments. Through this partnership, we've been able to enhance our product offerings, reduce time-to-market, and expand our customer base. The collaborative approach adopted by PILS has been a cornerstone of our innovation strategy, enabling us to leverage SAP's technological prowess to meet evolving market needs.

The market outlook for emSigner solutions, tailored for SAP customers, is exceptionally positive. As businesses worldwide accelerate their digital transformation journeys, the demand for secure, compliant, and efficient digital transaction management solutions is skyrocketing. We anticipate a significant increase in adoption among SAP customers across various industries, driven by the need to enhance operational efficiency, ensure compliance, and support remote work dynamics. With our strong partnership with SAP and continuous innovation, we are well-positioned to meet this growing demand and help businesses transform their digital document management practices. emSigner’s support for remote signing and embedded signing options on SAP platforms gives its users the flexibility to address high volume use-cases and security of their native application while performing signing of documents at scale.

Furthermore, emSigner's integration with SAP provides seamless user experiences and streamlined workflows, making it easier for organizations to implement and adopt digital signing processes across their operations. This compatibility is crucial for enterprises looking to leverage their SAP investment to the fullest, ensuring that digital transformation initiatives deliver maximum value. As the global marketplace continues to evolve towards more digital and remote-friendly work environments, emSigner's solutions stand at the forefront, offering SAP customers a robust platform that not only meets today's demands but is also scalable for future needs. This positions emSigner as a pivotal solution in the digital transformation strategies of businesses aiming for growth, sustainability, and competitive advantage in the digital age.

entry.title: Re: How to read Master Data Consolidation Process ID?

https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-blogs-by-members/how-to-custom-validation-rules-for-custom-entity-within-mdc/ba-p/13574725

entry.title: Re: Consumption for Component from DM not executed in S/4...
entry.content:
Hi Rahul,

we checked everything on site of DM - CPI - Cloud Connector. Everything seems to be okay here.

In the meantime we managed to post the goods issue via using the report CORUPROC in S/4. The report collects those "predefined records" and finally triggers the posting.

But we're not quite sure if this is working as intended? 

Thank you!

 

BR,

Christoph

entry.title: Re: I am not able to see Quality Action box in Q2 notific...

Hi,

Check configuration for Q2 notification in SPRO 

Regards

I'm going to give a try.

Thanks a lot!

entry.title: Integration of third party spell check
entry.content:
Hi guys,

I've found this blog post as I was searching for a spell check for CRM 7.0: 
https://community.sap.com/t5/customer-relationship-management-blogs-by-sap/spell-check-integration-with-sap-crm-webclient-and-interaction-center/ba-p/13084898

I would like to integrate the third party solution offered by JavaScriptSpellCheck.com.
Unfortunately I have not found a step by step guide to install this extension.
Does anyone has used this spell check before and could help me adding it to my Web UI?

Thanks

entry.title: Integrate ERMS with external API
entry.content:
Hello,

We have SAP ERMS solution build to route emails to agents using rules. 

Our organization has recently created an API which can better classify / extract intent/ keywords from email. We wanted to make use of this API in some of the rules if possible within ERMS 

Or

Once the current processing is done in ERMS and emails are allocated to a queue, we wanted to pull these and use the new API to classify, and based on the result allocate those to agents.

Our questions are 

1) Is it possible to externalize or augment the current rule builder with an external API ?

2) Are there rest APIs available to read emails out of a Queue and another API to allocate to a user ?

entry.title: SUBMIT
entry.content:
Hello Freinds,

After using SUBMIT i got error, please check and help me.

    DATA: lr_pay_data TYPE REF TO data,
          itab        TYPE REF TO data.
    FIELD-SYMBOLS: <lt_pay_data> TYPE table.

    cl_salv_bs_runtime_info=>set( EXPORTING display = abap_false
      metadata = abap_false
    data = abap_true ).

    SUBMIT zj3rflvmobvedh
    USING SELECTION-SCREEN 1000
          WITH so_bukrs EQ '1100'
          WITH so_budat IN s_budat
          WITH so_werks IN s_werks
          WITH so_matnr IN r_matnr
          AND RETURN.
    TRY.
        cl_salv_bs_runtime_info=>get_data_ref( IMPORTING r_data = lr_pay_data ).
        ASSIGN lr_pay_data->* TO <lt_pay_data>. "Standard report output is available in <lt_pay_data>
      CATCH cx_salv_bs_sc_runtime_info.
        MESSAGE `Unable to retrieve ALV data` TYPE 'E'.
    ENDTRY.
    cl_salv_bs_runtime_info=>clear_all( ).
    BREAK-POINT.
    LOOP AT <lt_pay_data> ASSIGNING FIELD-SYMBOL(<line>).

    ENDLOOP.

entry.title: Re: READ TABLE with OR condition
entry.content:
You could also perform two READ TABLEs, one for each value, and compare the timestamp to select the best record?

A nested LOOP could also be suitable, if you have an index (primary or secondary) to optimize it.

You need to run optimization tests if the size of your internal table is large.

entry.title: Back to basics with SAP Cloud Application Programming Model (CAP) - part 7
entry.content: In this episode, we look more closely at the Country type, and use that as a springboard to start exploring managed associations. Everyone welcome, especially beginners, come and join us for friendly chat and curiosity-driven learning-by-doing!

entry.title: Re: How to delete data from setup tables of 2LIS_02_SRV?
entry.content: duplicated comment

entry.title: Re: How to delete data from setup tables of 2LIS_02_SRV?
entry.content: Indeed, I did not pay attention to the suffix, I just looked at the application 02: 2003744 - 2LIS_02_SRV: How to extract package and line number of the sevice entry sheet

entry.title: Re: Display the data of an ABAP program or FM in a compos...
entry.content:
Hi Loed

 

In BW/4 maybe the BAdI Provider?

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_BW4HANA/107a6e8a38b74ede94c833ca3b7b6f51/9d157e8996714771bb11c1fab34a6412.html?locale=en-US

 

Or if you have BW 7.5 or below, you could create a SAPI extractor and consume it by an Open ODS View via ODP_SAPI.

 

br

Roland

entry.title: Re: READ TABLE with OR condition
entry.content:
Nested loops are only a problem if, as the tables increase in size linearly, the time taken increases rather more rapidly. I.e. if with twice as many records it takes more than twice as long. 

This can be avoid often by defining the tables as HASHED or SORTED with a good key.

entry.title: Re: E-Mail attached documents linked in QM02 (Quality Notifications)
entry.content:
For GOS attachment, try to use class CL_GOS_SERVICE_TOOLS with a method such as COPY_LINKED_OBJECTS.

* Attachement Creation Service
  ls_service-sign   = 'I'.
  ls_service-option = 'EQ'.
  ls_service-low    = 'PCATTA_CREA'.
  APPEND ls_service TO lt_services.
* Source object
  ls_source-instid = from_qmnum.
  ls_source-typeid = 'BUS2078'.
  ls_source-catid  = 'BO'.
* Target object
  ls_target-instid = to_qmnum.
  ls_target-typeid = 'BUS2078'.
  ls_target-catid  = 'BO'.
* Copy the objects between Source and Target
  cl_gos_service_tools=>copy_linked_objects(
      is_source            = ls_source
      is_target            = ls_target
      it_service_selection = lt_services
         ).
* Validate
  COMMIT WORK.

entry.title: Re: SUBMIT
entry.content:
Perhaps you'd like to share what the error was? 

Also, when you're putting code into a question, click on the ... to open up trhe options, and choose "HTML". Then your code will be nicely formatted.

entry.title: Re: Welcome to the beginner corner!
entry.content:
SAP Certified Development Associate - SAP Integration Suite 

 

Thank you David,

Thanks for your help and guidance in achieving my SAP certification. Much appreciated.

 

I wish All the very best for new learners exploring SAP path.

 

Thanks & Regards

Venkat

entry.title: Fixed Assets with Zero NBV not posting depreciation after increasing the Use life.
entry.content:
HI SAP Experts,

I have a number of assets which have had zero NBV for a couple of years. I want to increase their useful economic life (UEL) as we will be using them for 2 more years. In the past I have changed UEL in the asset master data, this has changed the planned depreciation in the asset explorer and then when AFAB is run it creates the depreciation posting. However, this has not worked this time. I have run AFAB + AFAR for the asset numbers hoping it triggers depreciation, but this has not worked. Any ideas how to resolve this. Many thanks in advance.

entry.title: Central Purchase Contracts: adjusting layout of the printed document template
entry.content:
Hello everyone,

can anyone help me with the question on how to adjust/ edit/ change the layout of the printed document template of a central purchase contract? With the transaction sfp it is not possible for me to change the layout of the standard form EV_CENTRAL_CONTRACT. Is there a how to adjust the form builder?

Thanks for your help!

Regards,

Bastian

entry.title: Re: ADT installation error - Eclipse 2023-12
entry.content:
I've only ever had problems when I've upgraded Eclipse. In those cases, I just do a clean re-install. 

You might also check you've got the latest JDK - I recommend SAP Machine (currently release 21).

entry.title: Re: SUBMIT
entry.content:
Here is the Error message.

 

Category ABAP programming error
Runtime Errors GETWA_NOT_ASSIGNED
ABAP Program ZFORMIV_REPORT_INGOTS
Application Component Not assigned
Date and Time 04.03.2024 15:24:12 (CET)
----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
|Short Text |
| Field symbol has not been assigned yet. |
----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
|What happened? |
| Error in the ABAP application program. |
| |
| The current ABAP program "ZFORMIV_REPORT_INGOTS" had to be terminated because |
| it found a |
| statement that could not be executed. |
----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
|Error analysis |
| An attempt was made to access a field symbol that has not been assigned |
| yet (data segment number "-1"). |
| |
| That error occurs if |
| - a typed field symbol is addressed before it has been set with ASSIGN, |
| or |
| - a field symbol is addressed that points to a row in an internal table |
| that has been deleted, or |
| - a field symbol is addressed that was previously reset using UNASSIGN, |
| or that pointed to a local field that no longer exists, or |
| - a parameter of a global function interface is address, although the |
| corresponding function module is not active (is not in the list of |
| active calls). The list of active calls can be taken from this short |
| dump. |
----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
|Trigger Location of Runtime Error |
| (Program) ZFORMIV_REPORT_INGOTS |
| (Include) ZFORMIV_REPORT_SEL_INGOTS |
| (Row) 455 |
| (Module Name) START-OF-SELECTION |
----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
|Source Code Extract |
----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
|Line |Code |
----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
| 425|* LOOP AT it_xtext. |
| 426|* IF sy-tabix >= 1. |
| 427|* SPLIT it_xtext-fields1 AT '|' INTO lv1 lv2 lv3. |
| 428|* ENDIF. |
| 429|* ENDLOOP. |
| 430| |
| 431| |
| 432| DATA: lr_pay_data TYPE REF TO data, |
| 433| itab TYPE REF TO data. |
| 434| FIELD-SYMBOLS: <lt_pay_data> TYPE table. |
| 435| |
| 436| cl_salv_bs_runtime_info=>set( EXPORTING display = abap_false |
| 437| metadata = abap_false |
| 438| data = abap_true ). |
| 439| |
| 440| SUBMIT zj3rflvmobvedh |
| 441| USING SELECTION-SCREEN 1000 |
| 442| WITH so_bukrs EQ '1100' |
| 443| WITH so_budat IN s_budat |
| 444| WITH so_werks IN s_werks |
| 445| WITH so_matnr IN r_matnr |
| 446| AND RETURN. |
| 447| TRY. |
| 448| cl_salv_bs_runtime_info=>get_data_ref( IMPORTING r_data = lr_pay_data ). |
| 449| ASSIGN lr_pay_data->* TO <lt_pay_data>. "Standard report output is available in <lt_|
| 450| CATCH cx_salv_bs_sc_runtime_info. |
| 451| MESSAGE `Unable to retrieve ALV data` TYPE 'E'. |
| 452| ENDTRY. |
| 453| cl_salv_bs_runtime_info=>clear_all( ). |
| 454| BREAK-POINT. |
|>>>>>| LOOP AT <lt_pay_data> ASSIGNING FIELD-SYMBOL(<line>). |
| 456| |
| 457| ENDLOOP. |
| 458|* End - Details from Tcode : zj3rflvmobvedh - on 26/Feb/2024 |
| 459| |
| 460| SELECT 'I' AS sign, |
| 461| 'EQ' AS option, |
| 462| low |
| 463| FROM tvarvc |
| 464| WHERE name = 'ZSSI_INWARD' |
| 465| INTO TABLE @r_ssingot_labor_type. |
| 466| |
| 467| IF r_ssingot_labor_type[] IS NOT INITIAL. |
| 468| SELECT matdoc~matnr, |
| 469| matdoc~bwart, |
| 470| matdoc~budat, |
| 471| matdoc~werks, |
| 472| matdoc~lifnr, |
| 473| matdoc~lgort, |
| 474| mara~labor, |
----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
|Active Calls/Events |
----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
|No. Ty. Program Include Line |
| Name |
----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
| 1 EVENT ZFORMIV_REPORT_INGOTS ZFORMIV_REPORT_SEL_INGOTS 455 |
| START-OF-SELECTION |
----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

entry.title: Role Overview in SAP PaPM Cloud Standard Model (Part 2/2)
entry.content:
Hi colleagues, welcome to the 2nd part of the blog post for the role overview in SAP Profitability and Performance Management Cloud Standard Model (SAP PaPM Cloud SM)!

On the previous blog post Role Overview in SAP PaPM Cloud Standard Model (Part 1/2), it discusses the role authorizations in the Environment, Modeling, Process Management, Report Management, Business Event Management, Show, Analyze and Visualize screens. I hope you already have an idea how it works.

With that, you might be curious about the remaining roles related to System and Administration screens. Let me invite you to read this blog post and see how the roles work on the mentioned screens.

To give you a little review, here are the major roles together with the new granular roles for System and Administration screens:

Yellow – For System screens.
Green – For Administration screens. 

For more information about these Roles, please refer to SAP Help Portal Roles Templates for SAP PaPM Cloud SM. 

On this blog post, I will be focusing on the Menu, System screens and Administration screens.

Without further ado, let’s proceed!

 

On the SAP PaPM Cloud SM, Menu button is always visible to the users that allows them to access the different System and Administration screens based on their assigned roles.

Let me provide you with a quick glimpse of it.

Based on the screenshot above, there are seven (7) options available in the Menu. Four (4) of which are the general options available for all users namely Help, User Preferences, About and Logout. While the remaining three (3) namely Home, System and Administration will be available based on the roles assigned to the users, which I will be discussing on the next sections.

Here’s the overview of the roles and its authorizations for Menu options:

An additional information, the Home option in the menu is equivalent to Environment screen.
For the Home/Environment screen role authorizations, it was discussed on the previous blogpost Role Overview in SAP PaPM Cloud Standard Model (Part 1/2).

Note:
To reiterate on how you can check the functionality of each role for the screens, inside the screenshots below, I have highlighted the buttons and indicated the roles in a tabular format for you to see the ability for each role. 
If a button (column e.g., Home) has a check mark next to a role (row e.g., ENVIRONMENTS_READ), it indicates that the role is capable of carrying out this particular action.  
If a button doesn’t have the check mark next to the role, then it means that the button is not visible, disabled or the user is not authorized (will return an unauthorized message).  

Environments and Modeling screen related roles:

Process, Reports and Business Event Management screen related roles:

System screens related roles:

Administration screens related roles:

To deep dive the roles per screen, let’s start first with the System Screens.

Application Monitor:

Process Monitor:

Comment Monitor:

Process Scheduler:

Modeling History:

Let’s continue with the Administration screens.

Teams:

Users:

Content Management:

Connections:

Settings:

Provisioning:

I hope you learned something new from this blog and shed some light when it comes to Roles in SAP Profitability and Performance Management Cloud.

Feel free to like and share this with your colleagues. And do follow the listed SAP community tags to be updated with the latest news for SAP PaPM!

  • PaPMCloudSM
  • PaPMCloudSMWhatsNew

Thank you for your time reading my blogpost and hope to see you on my next one!

entry.title: Re: queue returns multiple values (Graphical Mapping CPI)
entry.content: try to use "IF" instead of "IFs"

entry.title: Re: Using Fiori apps Project Cost Overview and Project Cost Line Items for Project Reporting
entry.content:
Hi @alexanderst, 

the virtual plan category is supported in the Project Cost Overview F6991 and Line Items F6992. In the Fin Reports like e.g. W0080 the virtual plan category is not supported, same is true for e.g. API for ACDOCP. This would require custom queries, the assignment of the category to the virtual plan category can be retrieved via I_VIRTUALPLANNINGCATEGORY. 

Regards, Andrea

entry.title: Re: Overview Page in SAP Business Application Studio using SAP's ES5 Service
entry.content:
Well, is there a quick way to include images?

I want external images, not the sap-icon custom images.

entry.title: Re: Not able to see my Badges after recent changes to com...
entry.content: @jerryjanda thanks for your reply... just following up on, because got email communication from community global that badges will be updated on March 1st. But they are still not updated in my profile... Thanks- Abhishek

entry.title: Re: I created an advanced excel file with abap ole object. But I want to send an e-mail instead of d
entry.content: OLE is not recommended if you have alternatives (because of its important drawbacks). Alternatives exist for Excel. OLE can work only in dialog mode, not in background. In dialog mode, you can save as a file, read it and attach it to an e-mail (send e-mails in ABAP the classic way)).

entry.title: Re: BTP Free Tier - hdi-shared entitlement also free
entry.content:
As per KBA 3416183 (Is there any cost for "hdi-shared" service plan in BTP cockpit?)

https://me.sap.com/notes/0003416183

service plan    description                                               free/paid
–––––––––––––––––––––--––––––––––––––––––––––––––––––––––––---––––––––--
hdi-shared      HDI container on a HANA database            free
schema          Schema on a HANA database                     free
securestore    User with permissions to use secure store   free

entry.title: Re: 🇨🇭 Getting Started with Machine Learning using SAP HANA (Lausanne, CH)
entry.content: There are essential prerequisites that must be completed by every attendee, before arriving at the event location.

entry.title: Re: Livestream of Next Generation SAP Enterprise Architect Forum
entry.content: I just missed it - will you share some recordings of the live event?

entry.title: Re: Production Operator Dashboard (POD) replacing XSTEPS...
entry.content:
Hello,

Have you got any answer regarding the subject.

Regards,

entry.title: Re: SAP MDK rule not triggered in List Picker search to h...
entry.content:
What platform are you seeing this issue? Android or iOS or both?

What MDK version you are currently on? e.g. 23.12.0

I tested the details you shared below on both iOS and Android (public store client) and see the expected results when searching.

entry.title: Re: Do I need to map new SD Routes also in EWM in an embe...
entry.content:
Hi,

Not sure if this is now posted twice, but this great new system of ours does not work as one would expect...

" Where can i do this in an embedded system...?"

As I wrote in my answer, the options have changed with the last release. So this node has moved from the "... for decentral EWM" to the delivery integration settings, and is valid for both deployment options.

Brgds

Juergen

entry.title: How to use ROUND( ) function to a nested case statement
entry.content:
Hi There!
I wrote the below case statement. But it is not giving the required results. I WANT 93,855 BUT I'M GETTING 938.5566666666666666666 like this. 

 

(SELECT CASE (MAIN.RECEIPT+MAIN.ISSUE) WHEN 0 THEN 0.0 ELSE MAIN.AMOUNT/(MAIN.RECEIPT+MAIN.ISSUE) END)  AS UNITP

 

So I tried writing the nested case statement for the above line and it worked but in the output, I'm getting so many numbers after decimal. I want the number to round off and I have tried ROUND( ) as well it is throwing an error maybe I misplaced the function. Can anyone let me know how to use ROUND( ) in nested case statements? Thanks

 

CASE
	WHEN 
		case MSEG.SHKZG when 'S' then MSEG.MENGE else 0.0 end
		+
		case MSEG.SHKZG when 'H' then MSEG.MENGE else 0.0 end = 0
	THEN 0.00
ELSE
	CASE
		WHEN 
			CASE WHEN MSEG.DMBTR = 0 THEN 0 ELSE 0.00 END = 0
			AND 
			CASE WHEN MSEG.MENGE = 0 THEN 0 ELSE 0.00 END = 0
			AND
			case MSEG.SHKZG when 'S' then MSEG.MENGE else 0.0 end = 0
			AND
			case MSEG.SHKZG when 'H' then MSEG.MENGE else 0.0 end = 0
		THEN 0
	ELSE
			MSEG.DMBTR/
			(case MSEG.SHKZG when 'S' then MSEG.MENGE else 0.0 end
			+
			case MSEG.SHKZG when 'H' then MSEG.MENGE else 0.0 end)
	END
END
AS UNITP,

entry.title: RCm - Approved Date of Job Requisition not Available or OData
entry.content:
Hello, 

My client cannot find the Job Requisition Approval Date field using OData API, whereas he can in Adhoc/Table Reporting or Stories report. 

I've found the following KBA: 2957351 - Approved Date of Job Requisition not Available in Integration Center or OData - Recruiting Management - SAP for Me

As this KBA is effectve dated in 2020, is it still true that the approved date is not an exposed field through API?

is the solution suggested on the KBA to add a custom field on the job requisition and to calculate it via a business rule still the best solution?

Has someone else experimented this request and has good practice to share to answer this request? 

Thank you, 

Marion

entry.title: Re: Do I need to map new SD Routes also in EWM in an embe...
entry.content:
Hi,

"One thing: I only saw a img activity in regards to activation in a decentral context. Where can i do this in an embedded system...?"

As I wrote, this has changed with SAP S/4HANA 2023. So with this release this node has moved from the "... for decentral EWM" part to the delivery integration settings part. Then it is valid for both, embedded and decentral.

Brgds

Juergen

entry.title: Re: How to connect with "Microsoft Power Apps" or "Micros...
entry.content: Yes, for the "push" you directly point at Integration Suite. Up to you if you need to front it with SAP APIM. For pull-based you may use the Dataverse Web API. That is plain OData and can be called with SAP's standard oData connector. No need for OpenConnectors. Alternatively, use Azure Data Factory to move the data from Dataverse to SAP

entry.title: Re: Adaptation Project Generator for VS Code - Adaptation...
entry.content: Hi, are you able to open https://ui5.sap.com in your browser? Also if you just do "curl https://ui5.sap.com", does it work?

entry.content:
Hi

Discount field was not showing in available fields in sales volume application.

Could you please suggest me.

entry.title: Re: First Program with AMDP method
entry.content: Great, Thanks for blog.

entry.title: Direct Cash flow report
entry.content:
Dear SAP Team,

We are currently in the process of demonstrating business scenarios to users in the UAE, Oman, Kuwait, and Saudi Arabia.

Our client is requesting a direct cash flow report from the system for these countries.


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: Direct Cash flow report
entry.content:
Dear Customer,

 

You can find the country-specific functions from the help portal link 

For the country UAE it is given that In United Arab Emirates, an enterprise should report its cash flow from its business activities in the indirect method, help portal link.

 

Simailarly Oman help portal link.

 

 

Please, find for Generic Cash Flow Statements help portal link  

 

Thanks & regards,

SAP Support

entry.title: Re: I created an advanced excel file with abap ole object...
entry.content: I can send an e-mail by trying this way. But if it happens like this, I need to download the file and add the file extension as Attachments. I want to send an e-mail without downloading the file. CREATE OBJECT outlook 'Outlook.Application'. CALL METHOD OF outlook 'CREATEITEM' = mailitem EXPORTING #1 = 0. GET PROPERTY OF mailitem 'Attachments' = lr_at. CALL METHOD OF lr_at 'Add' EXPORTING #1 = 'C:\Users\Regna123\Downloads\Test.xlsx'. * EXPORTING #1 = e_work. SET PROPERTY OF mailitem 'TO' = 'test@test.com.tr'. SET PROPERTY OF mailitem 'SUBJECT' = 'Test'. SET PROPERTY OF mailitem 'Body' = 'Some text'. * CALL METHOD OF mailitem 'ATTACHMENTS' = e_appl. CALL METHOD OF mailitem 'DISPLAY'. CALL METHOD OF mailitem 'SEND'. FREE OBJECT outlook.

entry.title: MF60 selection value problem
entry.content:
Hello, experts.

I can't add multiple values via MF60's selection areas.
For example :

I tried to enter the order number intervals.

After pressing "Enter", the field value changes to empty.

Also, using multiple selection area the field values couldn't select.
For example,


I want to select 2 order numbers. After pressing "Enter" buttons, the field values change to empty.
So, the pull list view is using to 1 or all orders.

Do you have any experience about this situation?

I hope, the situation is clear to you.
Waiting for the solutions soon.

Best regards

#MF60

entry.title: Re: February Developer Challenge - CAP Plugins: Week 1
Re: Adaptation Project Generator for VS Code - Adaptation...
entry.content: Yes, I can open https://ui5.sap.com without any problems in my browser and "curl https://ui5.sap.com" returns status code 200

entry.title: SAP CDP endpoint intent 'produce data to CDP' not saved
entry.content:
Hi all,

I'm creating a resource (endpoint) in SAP CDP connector studio. This resource needs to be triggered after the reception of a webhook trigger. The resource is needed to request more information about the incoming webhook request. 

Flow:

  1. Receive webhook from SAP Customer Data Cloud 
  2. Use UID from webhook, map it to the request schema of the new endpoint
  3. Send endpoint to get more info about the received UID (Customer) via an enrichment point.

This seems to be natural behaviour as written in SAP Help:

Create an enrichment point when an additional call is required to retrieve event information.

In some cases, such as when receiving webhook notifications, an incoming event can contain only some of the event information, and an additional call is required to retrieve the original event information from the source platform.

To make this configuration work, I need to register my endpoints intent as 'Producte data to CDP'. However when I save and publish this intent, I gets reverted to the option 'Consume data from CDP'.

When finally, I connect my newly created application as a source I get an internal CDP server error. The error is referring to the endpoint above mentioned.

 

{
  "callId": "72bed4070c8249dc95963c7e7200520e",
  "errorCode": 500000,
  "errorDetails": "Failed to finish application creation : Failed to create enrichment points: getAccountInfo",
  "errorMessage": "Internal Server Error",
  "apiVersion": 2,
  "statusCode": 500,
  "statusReason": "Internal Server Error",
  "time": "2024-03-01T15:18:12.162Z"
}

 

 

Could this be a bug in SAP Customer Data Platform itself? Or am I doing something wrong?

Thanks for your Help!

Eli

entry.title: Re: How to use attributes.ExampleCustomAttribute in a SAP BPA Process?
entry.content:
Hi @Dan_Wroblewski 

This helps a lot. Now I am able to understand the usage and functionality of custom attributes.

Thanks a lot 🙂

entry.title: The Power of AI-Driven Personalization in SAP Customer Experience
entry.content:
AI-driven personalization in SAP Customer Experience is transforming the way organizations understand and engage with their customers. By leveraging advanced AI algorithms, machine learning techniques, and data analytics, SAP CX empowers businesses to deliver highly relevant, contextualized experiences across every touchpoint of the customer journey.

At the heart of AI-driven personalization in SAP CX lies the ability to harness vast amounts of customer data to gain deep insights into individual preferences, behaviors, and needs. Through sophisticated data mining and analysis, SAP CX enables businesses to segment their customer base more effectively and identify actionable patterns and trends. This allows companies to create highly targeted marketing campaigns, promotions, and product recommendations that resonate with each customer on a personal level.

One of the key areas where AI-driven personalization is making a significant impact is in the realm of customer engagement and support. With SAP CX, businesses can deploy intelligent chatbots and virtual assistants that leverage natural language processing (NLP) and machine learning to provide personalized assistance and support to customers in real-time. These AI-powered bots can understand customer inquiries, anticipate their needs, and deliver relevant information or solutions instantly, enhancing the overall customer experience and driving customer satisfaction.

Moreover, AI-driven personalization in SAP CX extends beyond just marketing and support interactions—it also encompasses the entire product and service lifecycle. By analyzing customer data and feedback, SAP CX enables businesses to continuously refine and optimize their offerings to better meet the evolving needs and preferences of their customers. This iterative approach to product development and innovation ensures that companies stay ahead of the competition and deliver products and services that truly resonate with their target audience.

Furthermore, AI-driven personalization in SAP CX enables businesses to orchestrate seamless omnichannel experiences across multiple touchpoints and channels. Whether it's through a website, mobile app, social media, or in-store interactions, SAP CX ensures consistency and continuity in the customer experience journey. By leveraging AI-powered analytics and insights, businesses can track customer interactions across channels, identify cross-channel behaviors, and deliver personalized experiences that seamlessly transition from one touchpoint to another.

However, while AI-driven personalization in SAP CX offers immense opportunities for businesses to enhance customer engagement and drive growth, it also presents certain challenges and considerations. Chief among these is the need to maintain customer trust and privacy in an era of increasing data regulation and scrutiny. As businesses collect and analyze vast amounts of customer data to power AI-driven personalization, they must ensure that they are transparent about how customer data is being used and that they adhere to stringent data privacy regulations such as GDPR and CCPA.

In conclusion, AI-driven personalization in SAP Customer Experience represents a game-changer for businesses looking to elevate their customer engagement strategies to new heights. By leveraging the power of AI, machine learning, and data analytics, SAP CX enables organizations to deliver highly relevant, contextualized experiences that drive customer satisfaction, loyalty, and advocacy. As businesses continue to invest in AI-driven personalization, the ability to understand, anticipate, and meet the needs of individual customers will become a defining factor in their success in the digital age.

entry.title: Re: Adding new fields to Characteristics catalogue.
entry.content:
Hi,

You have to use custom fields and logic app for this. Also before creating new custom field with this app you should check the restriction note about aATP. For example custom field with check box type does not supported in ABC catalogue. 

Thanks,

entry.title: Re: Adding new fields to Characteristics catalogue.
entry.content:
Hi,

You have to use custom fields and logic app for this. Also before creating new custom field with this app you should check the restriction note about aatp. For example custom field with check box type does not supported in ABC catalogue.

Thanks,

entry.title: Re: How to use attributes.ExampleCustomAttribute in a SAP BPA Process?
entry.content:
Hi @Dinu ,

Thanks for your inputs. The reply from both you and Dan has clarified my doubt.

Reagrds,

Karthika

entry.title: Re: Do I need to map new SD Routes also in EWM in an embe...
entry.content: ok, i see. thx

entry.title: Configuring RE Accrual engine
entry.content:
I am configuring the RE accrual engine and have a few questions.

First, I’ll give some info about the requirement:

 

- Client will create Lease-in, service and insurance contracts

- Payments for more than one period are made in advance (deferrals)

- Lease-in contracts are valuated according to IFRS (main ledger) and German GAAP (non-leading ledger).

 

I have done all the customizing steps of consulting note 829676 and tested two contracts.

 

  • First contract - valuation relevant:

 1. TRCOCN stand for contract accrual and TRCORO for the object (in this case the cost center). Why does the system create two accrual objects in this case? It doesn’t seem correct to me since the payment of 20000 € is recognized twice.

I tried to deactivate the postings to accrual type TRCORO, but the accrual run doesn’t work by doing so.

Could someone explain if this is a correct behavior, and if yes, why is the object posting important?

 

 2. Although the contract is valuated as per IFRS, accruals are posted only to HGB (non-leading ledger). As per note 2441863 it is the correct behavior, since German GAAP is the Acct. Principle assigned to the transfer posting valuation rule:

That would mean that the no accrual objects are created for IFRS-valuated contracts.

Which effects does this have from an accounting perspective? Is there any way to extend this postings to IFRS as well?

  • Second contract – non-valuation relevant

3. Accrual run post accrual object to IFRS, which seems correct to me. I can observe that the Accrual transaction U is used instead of I. Could you give me feedback on this posting behavior as well? Are transactions C and F also necessary for deferrals?

 

In general, the client wants to post deferrals to both accounting principles. I suppose that is only possible using an BadI, right?

I’d also appreciate if you could give me some explanation on why accrual engine needs a start account and target account (that may differ from each other). There is SAP document with some accrual/deferral examples, but they are way much different than the posting results I achieved with the contracts above.

 

Thank you very much.

 

Regards

Thaís

entry.title: ABAP RAP - How to refresh the list of childrens after an root action has created a new child
entry.content:
I have a root-child data model (User-\_ChangeRequest), where childrens can be created through an action from the object page of the root entity.

I tried many scenarios already, but in no one of these the list of child nodes gets reloaded, after the action has created a new child:

action with result [1] $self

 

 

define behavior for Z_R_User alias User
{
...
  action ( features:instance ) createChangeRequest result [1] $self;
...
}

 

 

Filling the result parameter from the action

 

 

READ ENTITIES OF Z_R_User IN LOCAL MODE
     ENTITY User
     ALL FIELDS
     WITH VALUE #( FOR mapped_request IN mapped-changerequest
                 ( %tky = CORRESPONDING #( mapped_request-%tky ) ) )
     RESULT DATA(update_result).

result = VALUE #( FOR user IN update_result
                  ( %tky   = CORRESPONDING #( user-%tky )
                    %param = user ) ).

 

 

 action with result [1] entity Z_R_ChangeRequest

 

 

define behavior for Z_R_User alias User
{
...
  action ( features : instance ) createChangeRequest result [1] entity Z_R_ChangeRequest;
...
}

 

 

 Filling the result parameter from the action

 

 

READ ENTITIES OF Z_R_User IN LOCAL MODE         
     ENTITY ChangeRequest                                    
     ALL FIELDS                                              
     WITH VALUE #( FOR mapped_request IN mapped-changerequest
                   ( %tky = mapped_request-%tky ) )          
     RESULT DATA(update_result).                             
                                                             
result = VALUE #( FOR request IN update_result               
                  ( %tky   = CORRESPONDING #( request-%tky ) 
                    %param = request ) ).

 

 

 

factory action 

 

 

define behavior for Z_R_User alias User
{
...
  factory action ( features : instance ) createChangeRequest [1];
...
}

 

 

 I had to fill the table user in the changing parameter mapped manually, because if not the following error appears:

 

 

 

LOOP AT mapped-changerequest INTO DATA(request).                 
  mapped-user = VALUE #( BASE mapped-user                        
                         ( %tky = CORRESPONDING #( request-%tky )
                           %cid = request-%cid ) ).              
ENDLOOP.

 

 

But as mentioned before, no scenario get the child list updated after the action.

The function "side effects" has the option, that a entity will be reloaded after an action, but the feature is not available on our on-premise 2022 S/4HANA system:

action: Whenever the action is executed on the UI, the side effect is triggered and the defined targets are reloaded.

side effects { action MyAction affects Targets }

https://help.sap.com/docs/abap-cloud/abap-rap/side-effects#types-of-side-effects

Has anyone a suggestion how to achive this behavior?

entry.title: Guideline values not updated even after we change it
entry.content:
Hi All,


We have guideline setup in variable Pay template. After we launched default values are populating in the field. However when we change the value to some other value and save. It is not getting saved and default value is appearing again. What could be the reason here.

PFB example.
Guideline min-default-max -> 10-20-30
after launch Guideline is updated as 20 in worksheet. When manager is changing it to 25 and save, it is again defaulting to 20 again. 

Guideline optimization is selected, Force Default On Rating Change is NO, Force Default On Custom Field Change is NO.

Regards

entry.title: Re: Enable Now video generated but audio is missing
entry.content: Hi @ShaneLipke

entry.title: Re: Enable Now video generated but audio is missing
entry.content: Hi @ShaneLipke ... I have the same issue, Is there a resolution to this issue? The video after text to speech in a simulation plays in a preview, but not after generating the video.

entry.title: Re: February Developer Challenge - CAP Plugins: Week 1
Re: Closed DO Item with Invoice Number Query
entry.content: SELECT T0."DocDate", T0.DocEntry, T0.[DocNum] 'DO No', T0.[CardCode], T0.[CardName], T1.[VisOrder] + 1 'LineNo.', T1.[ItemCode], T1.[Dscription], T1.[Quantity], T1.[OpenQty], T2.Telephone as 'Sales Employee', T1.[TargetType], T1.[TrgetEntry], T4.DocNum FROM ODLN T0 INNER JOIN DLN1 T1 ON T0.[DocEntry] = T1.[DocEntry] INNER JOIN OSLP T2 ON T0.[SlpCode] = T2.[SlpCode] LEFT OUTER JOIN INV1 T3 on T1.[TargetType]=T3.[TrgetEntry] LEFT OUTER JOIN OINV T4 ON T4.[DocEntry] = T1.[TrgetEntry] WHERE T0."DocStatus" = 'C'

entry.title: Re: ABAP RAP - How to refresh the list of childrens after...
entry.content:
Hi,

 

the list should be refreshed when you hand over the right object after it was created. You should look deeper into your ABAP coding.

 

Another way to achieve refresh (which I would recommend only if you come from the frontend - so you have triggered some special logic on the frontend side and you want to refresh it):

https://sapui5.hana.ondemand.com/sdk/#/api/sap.suite.ui.generic.template.ObjectPage.extensionAPI.ExtensionAPI%23methods/refresh

 

You could implement such a breakout coding by using the refresh method on the extensionAPI.

 

Best,

Edrilan Berisha

SAP S/4HANA Cloud Financials Development

entry.title: Re: Closed DO Item with Invoice Number Query
entry.content: I managed to modify my Query and get the result

entry.title: Re: Cannnot copy journal entry in Manange journal entry.
entry.content:
Hi,

 

there are several limitations to it, some are certain fields others are already mentioned in this question thread:

https://community.sap.com/t5/enterprise-resource-planning-q-a/how-to-copy-an-existing-journal-entry/qaq-p/12486560

 

Best,

Edrilan Berisha

SAP S/4HANA Cloud Financials Development

entry.title: Re: CAN'T EDIT SHOPPING CART BY ADDING ANOTHER ITEM
entry.content:
Hi DhellCenina,

has your issue been resolved? Most likely it's related to your application, not to sap business client. What application are you using? Please find out and post your question directly there. Depending on your application you could also test it using the browser only and see what happens. If it works within the browser standalone but not inside sap business client - come back to me. 

Regards, 

Sandra

entry.title: Re: call unbound RAP action from CAP
entry.content:
I have found a possible solution, but I can imagine that this is what the author had in mind.

await remote.send('POST', 'service.entity/namespace.action', {parameters)

I am very open to further approaches/ideas.

entry.title: HI All I am very new SLT.how can we supress leading zeros
entry.content:
Hi Team,

I am New SLT how can we Suppress leading zeros in SLT  replication? we have sources system S/4 Hana in-built SLT. Target SAP HANA on cloud, we see in these fields when data is being written to ACDOCA table in SAP HANA on cloud. We have master table field length 15 actual value 12345 but when SLT replication form source to target we sseeing additional zero add before the actual value of 000012345 is any way to fix this issue.

entry.title: Re: How to query for bin location/warehouse history for i...

entry.title: Factoring in SAP ERP (ECC)
entry.content:
Hi,

Does anyone know whether Factoring (hidden one) like explained in S/4HANA documentation: https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_ON-PREMISE/c87bf7233f604208a82764b738156247/aa27940391e64154b0522701639d04d6.html

is also available in SAP ECC via Settlement Management ?

Thanks

entry.title: Re: By default Save of HR Manager in Job Relationship portlet in absence of Position Management.
entry.content:
Great tip but I have a doubt, when you change the Org Data of an employee the information about the HR Manager on the job relationship changes automatically, or do you have to enter in the portlet and make some changes for the rule to run and find the new HR Manager?

I did all the processes you mentioned before but when I change the Org Data of an employee I have to open the job relationship portlet for the information to actualize
